prevalence.trend: stable, with slight increase due to rising vegetarian/vegan diets

riskFactors: Chronic blood loss from ulcers or cancer
Vegetarian or vegan diets without supplementation
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: WHO/EFSA criteria: Hb <12 g/dL women, <13 g/dL men, ferritin <15 μg/L

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: routine prenatal screening in all EU countries
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 75% among diagnosed cases, supported by comprehensive prenatal care

regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Oral iron (ferrous sulfate, ferrous fumarate), intravenous iron in severe cases, dietary counseling
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Lower prevalence than global average (30-40%), disparities in Eastern EU countries, increasing vegetarian/vegan diets
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Universal prenatal screening, high-quality diagnostic access, food fortification voluntary in some countries
